key_insight: LiL-Q turns nonlinear PDE solving into a sequence of convex linear least-squares problems by combining quasilinearization with physics-informed neural networks whose parameters enter linearly.
engineering_relevance: It offers a more stable and parameter-efficient alternative to standard PINN training for difficult forward PDE problems in fluid flow, elasticity, porous media, and reaction-diffusion systems.
method: Bellman-Kalaba quasilinearization linearizes the nonlinear PDE at each outer step.
Each linear subproblem is solved by direct QR-based least-squares on a Linear-in-Learnables trial space.
The trial space can use random features, spectral polynomials, or trigonometric bases within a PINN formulation.
evidence_signal: The authors report single-digit outer iterations on seven benchmarks, machine-precision recovery when the exact solution lies in the trial space, and up to two orders of magnitude fewer trainable parameters than compared PINN solvers on Navier-Stokes cases.
watch_item: Convergence is local and depends on a smallness condition plus the approximation power of the chosen trial space, so performance may degrade if the initial guess or basis is inadequate.
paper_title: A Convex Quasilinearization Method for Solving Nonlinear PDEs with Physics-Informed Neural Networks
